subject: [PATCH v3 3/3] fuse: use iomap helper to mark folio uptodate

2026-07-07.patch_summary: This patch updates the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) code to use a new helper function, iomap_folio_mark_uptodate(), when marking folios as uptodate. This is necessary because when large folios are enabled, the uptodate state of an iomap-backed folio can become out of sync if only the folio-level flag is updated. The patch addresses this issue by using the new helper function to update both the folio uptodate state and the iomap uptodate bitmap. This change ensures that the uptodate state remains consistent across both the folio and the iomap, even when large folios are enabled.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Identified the need for updating both the folio-level flag and the iomap uptodate bitmap when marking a folio uptodate. Confirmed that using the iomap helper addresses this issue.

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Acknowledged the patch as a solution to the problem of updating both the folio-level flag and the iomap uptodate bitmap.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, 8 Jul 2026 at 00:05, Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> When fuse enables large folios, a large folio will be backed by
> iomap_folio_state that keeps track of uptodate and dirty state in an
> internal bitmap.
>
> Fuse writethrough and notify store paths currently set folio uptodate
> state with folio_mark_uptodate(), which touches only the folio-level
> flag, but on an iomap-backed folio, that leaves the uptodate bitmap out
> of sync.
>
> Use the iomap_folio_mark_uptodate() helper to update both the folio
> uptodate state and the iomap uptodate bitmap.
>
> Reviewed-by: Darrick J. Wong <djwong@kernel.org>
> Signed-off-by: Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com>

Acked-by: Miklos Szeredi <mszeredi@redhat.com>

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch series updates the FUSE file system to use the iomap helper for marking folios uptodate when large folios are enabled. The change ensures that both the folio-level flag and the internal bitmap of an iomap-backed folio are updated correctly, preventing potential issues with data consistency.
